pattern: The degree of a vertex in an adjacency matrix is just how many ones sit in its own row, so summing each row directly gives every vertex's answer.
hints: The adjacency matrix already tells you, row by row, exactly which vertices each vertex connects to.
For a given vertex, its degree is simply the count of 1s appearing in its row of the matrix.
Compute this row sum independently for every vertex to build the full answer array.

edge_inputs.description: a fully connected triangle of three vertices
a mix of connected and isolated vertices
a single vertex with no possible edges
a completely isolated set of vertices with no edges at all
a vertex connected to every other vertex has the maximum possible degree
a simple path graph among four vertices

stress.description: random symmetric adjacency matrices across the full vertex-count bound
